Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Deposition Testimony Of The Custodian Of Records And Person(S) Most Qualified Of The S.F. District Atty'S Office
Off Calendar Apr-25-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Bryan Vereschagin for plaintiff and moving party: Stancy Nesby
Peter J. Keith and Celia W. Lee defendant and opposing party: City and County of San Francisco
Wayne K. Snodgrass for defendant and opposing party: San Francisco District Attorney
HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Stancy Nesby's motion to compel production of documents and deposition testimony of the person most knowledgeable and custodian of records of the San Francisco District Attorney.
Granted in part. The motion is granted with the limitation that the District Attorney may redact the following types of information:
1) attorney work product;
2) notes, memoranda, chronological records of investigation, or other documents, that are unrelated to the investigation of the identity of the defendant in San Francisco Superior Court #173690 and 176799, Stancy Nesby and Meseret K. Abraha.
3) Personal identifying information of any non-law enforcement witness, crime victim, and informant;
This order does not apply to police incident reports relating to Stancy Nesby and Meseret K. Abraha, provided that any personal identifying information of any non-law enforcement witness, crime victim, and informant may be redacted from said reports and not subject to disclosure absent a showing of good cause. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Depostion Testimony From Linda Hackett And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against The City And County Of San Francscio, Peter Keith, Celia Lee
Off Calendar Apr-25-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Bryan Vereschagin for plaintiff and moving party: Stancy Nesby
Peter J. Keith and Celia W. Lee defendant and opposing party: City and County of San Francisco
Wayne K. Snodgrass for defendant and opposing party: San Francisco District Attorney

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Stancy Nesby's motion to compel further responses to questions pursuant to CCP Section 2025.480 is granted in part and denied in part. The witness is ordered to respond to the question of whether she has been subject to any corrective action in relation to the booking or fingerprinting of Stancy Nesby, and limited follow-up questions as to the nature of the corrective action, if any. The motion is denied as to the other questions. Information as to the witness' job performance unrelated to the booking or fingerprinting of Stancy Nesby is irrelevant to the subject matter of this action and protected by the witness' right to privacy.

The Court will consider the motion relating to the NCIC manual, the AFIS and NATMS documents, and the changes to the witness' testimony as brought pursuant to CCP Section 2025.610. The motion is denied as the Court does not find good cause to order a subsequent deposition. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
